Entire CLI will link this conversation to your next commit.
  1 other active conversation(s) in this workspace will also be included.
  Use 'entire status' for more information.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# WP-043 — IMG-V3-01: Image → layout JSON (deterministic CV)
2	
3	**Branch:** `feat/v3-img-v3-01-image-to-layout`
4	**Effort:** L — 3 weeks (deterministic CV is non-trivial; OCR integration adds a week)
5	**Status:** `now`
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Required reading
10	
11	Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.
12	
13	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §3 Design Pillars + anti-patterns A1–A10 (lines 73–144); §6 T9 IMG-V3-01 entry (lines 3243–3278); §A Agent Instructions (lines 3842–3910)
14	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
15	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/skb/calibrator.py` — SKB-04 deterministic calibrator (v2 prior)
16	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/skb/edge_detection.py` — SKB-14 edge detection (v2 prior)
17	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/skb/colour_sampler.py` — SKB-07 region colour sampler (v2 prior)
18	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/commands.py` — `Command` union; add `TraceImageCmd` here
19	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine.py` — command dispatch; add handler here
20	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/api/registry.py` — tool descriptor registry (API-V3-01 contract)
21	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/cli/cli.mjs` — CLI entry point; register `bim-ai trace` here
22	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/jobs/queue.py` — Jobs queue (JOB-V3-01); large traces run as jobs
23	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/jobs/types.py` — Job type definitions
24	
25	---
26	
27	## Verbatim IMG-V3-01 tracker entry (§6 T9, line 3243)
28	
29	**IMG-V3-01 — Image-input → structured-layout tool (deterministic CV)**
30	
31	_Source: R-A §8 (AI tracing image → vector); R-C §2 + §4 (AutoLayout multi-modal input). Reframed entirely from "agent traces sketch via multimodal AI" to "**deterministic CV** tool that produces a structured layout an external agent can read." The wire-format JSON is the contract; the CV implementation is swappable._
32	
33	**Status.** `now` for the deterministic CV path. `vision` for AI-swappable upgrades.
34	**Scope.** A deterministic CV tool — line detection, polygon recovery, optional OCR for dimensions — that takes an image and returns a structured layout JSON. **No AI runs inside the binary.** Pipeline:
35	
36	1. **Preprocessing.** SKB-04 calibrator + SKB-14 edge detection + SKB-07 region colour sampler (v2 primitives).
37	2. **Polygon recovery.** Hough line detection → contour fitting → room-region segmentation. Pure CV.
38	3. **Dimension OCR (optional).** Tesseract on labelled strings; rejected gracefully if illegible.
39	4. **Layout JSON emission.** Returns a `StructuredLayout` round-trippable into TKN-V3-01's `EnvelopeToken[]`.
40	
41	The output JSON is the wire format an external agent reads. The agent decides how to interpret it — refine with its own vision, call CMD-V3-01 directly, combine with a brief. The CV is the **floor**: a deterministic baseline that always works. AI augmentations are external and optional.
42	
43	**Data model.** New `StructuredLayout` type designed to be both human-debuggable and TKN-V3-01-compatible:
44	```ts
45	type StructuredLayout = {
46	  schemaVersion: 'img-v3.0';
47	  imageMetadata: { widthPx: number; heightPx: number; calibrationMmPerPx?: number };
48	  rooms: Array<{
49	    id: string;
50	    polygonMm: Array<{ x: number; y: number }>;
51	    detectedTypeKey?: string;                // CV-detected room type from glyph / colour
52	    detectedAreaMm2?: number;
53	  }>;
54	  walls: Array<{ id: string; aMm: { x: number; y: number }; bMm: { x: number; y: number }; thicknessMm?: number }>;
55	  openings: Array<{ id: string; hostWallId: string; tAlongWall: number; widthMm?: number; kindHint?: 'door' | 'window' }>;
56	  ocrLabels: Array<{ text: string; bboxMm: { x: number; y: number; w: number; h: number }; confidence: number }>;
57	  advisories: Advisory[];                    // 'low_contrast_image', 'no_dimensions_detected', etc.
58	};
59	```
60	**Engine.** New CLI: `bim-ai trace --image <path> [--brief <path>] [--archetype-hint <key>] -o layout.json`. Runs SKB-04 + SKB-14 + SKB-07 (deterministic) + polygon recovery + optional OCR. Output: `(StructuredLayout, advisories)`. Registered as a tool descriptor per API-V3-01. The brief is passed through as metadata only — the deterministic CV does not interpret it; agents that read the brief do their own thing with it.
61	**UI.** Drop zone on canvas; "Trace as new model" enqueues a job. On completion, side-by-side preview of the source image + a SVG render of the structured layout. The user can manually correct the layout (edit walls, re-label rooms) before handing off to an agent for downstream interpretation.
62	**Acceptance.** (a) Drop `target-house-vis-colored.png`; trace completes in <10 s wall-clock on a single CPU; output `StructuredLayout` JSON contains ≥10 rooms and ≥40 walls. (b) Same image dropped twice → byte-identical JSON output (deterministic). (c) `bim-ai trace --image low-contrast.png` returns a layout with `low_contrast_image` advisory + degraded-but-sane polygons. (d) An external agent harness reads the layout JSON, calls CMD-V3-01 to seed the model, kernel reconstructs a valid model with green soundness pack.
63	**Effort.** L — 3 weeks (deterministic CV is non-trivial; OCR integration adds a week).
64	**Cross-theme references.** **API-V3-01 (registered tool).** **TKN-V3-01 (output JSON is round-trippable into envelope tokens).** **T6 (image-side primitives — IMG-V3-01 is the bridge from T6 to the kernel).** **JOB-V3-01 (large traces run as jobs).** **v2 SKB-04, SKB-07, SKB-14 (deterministic priors).**
65	
66	---
67	
68	## Setup
69	
70	```bash
71	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
72	git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
73	git checkout -b feat/v3-img-v3-01-image-to-layout
74	python -c "from bim_ai.skb.calibrator import calibrate; print('skb ok')"
75	uv sync && pnpm install
76	```
77	
78	---
79	
80	## Implementation rules (verbatim from §A)
81	
82	- **Honour API-V3-01.** Every kernel command you add must have CLI + REST/RPC + JSON schema + assumption-log support. Add tests at the agent-callable layer (`tests/cli/`, `tests/api/`).
83	- **No scope creep.** Do not silently add features not in the tracker. v3 is a 4–6 month commitment — scope creep is the largest risk. If a requirement would expand §L Non-Goals, stop and escalate.
84	- **Design-pillar adherence.** Do not skim §3 Design Pillars or the anti-pattern catalog (A1–A10). A WP that violates D1 (direct manipulation first) or A9 (always-visible Properties panel) ships incomplete.
85	- **No hex literals in `packages/web/src/**/*.tsx`.** Use CSS token variables only. The ESLint rule (R-G §2.5 phase 3) will fail builds that introduce hex literals.
86	- **No AI runs inside the binary.** The `bim-ai trace` CLI is pure deterministic CV. The wire-format JSON is the contract; AI augmentations are external and optional.
87	- **Determinism is a hard requirement.** Same image in → byte-identical JSON out. Every intermediate CV step must be seeded / stateless. CI will call trace twice on the same input and diff the outputs.
88	- **Use JOB-V3-01 for large images.** Any trace that takes >2 s wall-clock must be dispatched through the jobs queue and cancellable on user request.
89	
90	---
91	
92	## Implementation tasks
93	
94	### 1. New module `app/bim_ai/img/`
95	
96	Create the package:
97	
98	```
99	app/bim_ai/img/__init__.py
100	app/bim_ai/img/types.py        # StructuredLayout + Advisory Pydantic models
101	app/bim_ai/img/pipeline.py     # trace() entry point
102	app/bim_ai/img/polygon.py      # Hough line → contour → room segmentation
103	app/bim_ai/img/ocr.py          # Tesseract wrapper (graceful fallback)
104	```
105	
106	`types.py` — define `StructuredLayout`, `RoomRegion`, `WallSegment`, `OpeningHint`, `OcrLabel`, `Advisory` as Pydantic models matching the data model above. `schemaVersion` is a `Literal['img-v3.0']` field.
107	
108	`pipeline.py` — `trace(image_path: str, *, archetype_hint: str | None = None, brief_path: str | None = None) -> StructuredLayout`. Calls SKB-04 calibrator → SKB-14 edge detection → SKB-07 colour sampler → `polygon.recover_rooms()` → `ocr.extract_labels()`. Brief is attached as `imageMetadata.briefPath` string only — not interpreted. All CV steps must be deterministic (no random seeds, no probabilistic models).
109	
110	`polygon.py` — `recover_rooms(edges, calibration) -> tuple[list[RoomRegion], list[WallSegment], list[OpeningHint]]`. Implements Hough line detection → contour fitting → room segmentation. Use `opencv-python` (already in constraints; no new deps without asking).
111	
112	`ocr.py` — `extract_labels(image, calibration) -> list[OcrLabel]`. Wraps `pytesseract`; on `ImportError` or Tesseract-not-installed, returns `[]` and appends `Advisory(code='tesseract_unavailable')`. Never raises.
113	
114	### 2. CLI command (`packages/cli/cli.mjs`)
115	
116	Register `bim-ai trace`:
117	
118	```
119	bim-ai trace --image <path> [--brief <path>] [--archetype-hint <key>] [-o <output.json>]
120	```
121	
122	- Calls `POST /api/v3/trace` (for large images: enqueues a job via JOB-V3-01 queue; for small images: runs inline).
123	- Prints `StructuredLayout` JSON to stdout (or writes to `-o` path).
124	- Exit code 0 on success; exit code 1 if the layout contains a fatal advisory (`no_walls_detected`).
125	
126	### 3. API tool descriptor (`app/bim_ai/api/registry.py`)
127	
128	Register `img_trace` tool per API-V3-01 contract: `{ name, description, inputSchema (JSON Schema for TraceImageCmd), outputSchema (JSON Schema for StructuredLayout) }`.
129	
130	### 4. REST route (`app/bim_ai/routes_api.py` or new `routes_trace.py`)
131	
132	`POST /api/v3/trace` — accepts `multipart/form-data` with `image` file + optional `brief` file + optional `archetypeHint` string. For images >2 MB, enqueues a `TraceJob` via the jobs queue and returns `{ jobId }`. For images ≤2 MB, runs inline and returns `StructuredLayout` JSON directly.
133	
134	### 5. `TraceImageCmd` (`app/bim_ai/commands.py`)
135	
136	```python
137	class TraceImageCmd(BaseModel):
138	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
139	    type: Literal["TraceImage"] = "TraceImage"
140	    image_b64: str = Field(alias="imageB64")          # base64-encoded image bytes
141	    archetype_hint: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="archetypeHint")
142	    brief_text: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="briefText")
143	    assumptions: list = Field(default_factory=list)   # API-V3-01 assumption log
144	```
145	
146	Add to the `Command` union. Engine handler calls `img.pipeline.trace()` and returns the `StructuredLayout` as the command result (no kernel mutation — trace is a read-only analysis tool).
147	
148	### 6. TypeScript types (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)
149	
150	```ts
151	export type Advisory = {
152	  code: string;
153	  message?: string;
154	};
155	
156	export type StructuredLayout = {
157	  schemaVersion: 'img-v3.0';
158	  imageMetadata: { widthPx: number; heightPx: number; calibrationMmPerPx?: number };
159	  rooms: Array<{
160	    id: string;
161	    polygonMm: Array<{ x: number; y: number }>;
162	    detectedTypeKey?: string;
163	    detectedAreaMm2?: number;
164	  }>;
165	  walls: Array<{ id: string; aMm: { x: number; y: number }; bMm: { x: number; y: number }; thicknessMm?: number }>;
166	  openings: Array<{ id: string; hostWallId: string; tAlongWall: number; widthMm?: number; kindHint?: 'door' | 'window' }>;
167	  ocrLabels: Array<{ text: string; bboxMm: { x: number; y: number; w: number; h: number }; confidence: number }>;
168	  advisories: Advisory[];
169	};
170	```
171	
172	### 7. UI: image drop zone (`packages/web/src/plan/ImageTraceDropZone.tsx`)
173	
174	- Accepts `.png`, `.jpg`, `.jpeg`, `.pdf` via drag-and-drop or file picker.
175	- On drop: `POST /api/v3/trace` with `multipart/form-data`. If response is `{ jobId }`, tracks the job via JOB-V3-01; if response is `StructuredLayout`, renders immediately.
176	- On completion: side-by-side preview — source image on the left, SVG render of detected walls/rooms on the right.
177	- SVG render uses `--draft-lw-cut-major` for walls and `--color-surface-strong` for room fills at 0.15 opacity.
178	- No hex literals. All colours via CSS token variables.
179	
180	### 8. Tests (`app/tests/test_img_v3_01.py`)
181	
182	Cover:
183	
184	- `trace()` on a synthetic PNG with a simple rectangle → `StructuredLayout` with ≥1 room and ≥4 walls.
185	- `trace()` called twice on the same file → byte-identical JSON output (determinism gate).
186	- Low-contrast image (flat grey PNG) → layout contains `low_contrast_image` advisory; polygons list is non-empty (degraded-but-sane).
187	- `ocr.extract_labels()` when Tesseract unavailable → returns `[]` + `tesseract_unavailable` advisory; does not raise.
188	- `TraceImageCmd` dispatched through engine → returns a dict with `schemaVersion == 'img-v3.0'`.
189	- `TraceImageCmd` with no `assumptions` field → still accepted (assumption log is optional for analysis-only tools).
190	
191	---
192	
193	## Verify gate
194	
195	```bash
196	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app
197	python -m pytest tests/test_img_v3_01.py -v
198	python -m pytest tests/ -x -q
199	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
200	pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
201	pnpm format:check
202	make verify
203	```
204	
205	If `make verify` does not exist, run `pnpm test` from the repo root instead and confirm it exits 0.
206	
207	---
208	
209	## Commit and push
210	
211	Stage specific files only — do not use `git add -A` or `git add .`.
212	
213	```bash
214	git add app/bim_ai/img/__init__.py \
215	        app/bim_ai/img/types.py \
216	        app/bim_ai/img/pipeline.py \
217	        app/bim_ai/img/polygon.py \
218	        app/bim_ai/img/ocr.py \
219	        app/bim_ai/commands.py \
220	        app/bim_ai/engine.py \
221	        app/bim_ai/api/registry.py \
222	        app/tests/test_img_v3_01.py \
223	        packages/cli/cli.mjs \
224	        packages/core/src/index.ts \
225	        packages/web/src/plan/ImageTraceDropZone.tsx
226	git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
227	feat(img): IMG-V3-01 image → layout JSON (deterministic CV)
228	
229	New bim_ai/img/ pipeline: SKB-04 calibrator + SKB-14 edge detection +
230	SKB-07 colour sampler → Hough polygon recovery + Tesseract OCR (graceful
231	fallback) → StructuredLayout JSON. Registered as API-V3-01 tool; CLI
232	bim-ai trace; POST /api/v3/trace with JOB-V3-01 queue for >2 MB images;
233	ImageTraceDropZone side-by-side preview component.
234	
235	Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
236	EOF
237	)"
238	git push -u origin feat/v3-img-v3-01-image-to-layout
239	```
240	
241	Do NOT open a PR or merge to main.
242	
243	---
244	
245	## Final report template
246	
247	Paste this block back verbatim with all fields filled in:
248	
249	```
250	WP: IMG-V3-01

252	Branch: feat/v3-img-v3-01-image-to-layout

# WP-043 — IMG-V3-01: Image → layout JSON (deterministic CV)

**Branch:** `feat/v3-img-v3-01-image-to-layout`
**Effort:** L — 3 weeks (deterministic CV is non-trivial; OCR integration adds a week)
**Status:** `now`

---

## Required reading

Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §3 Design Pillars + anti-patterns A1–A10 (lines 73–144); §6 T9 IMG-V3-01 entry (lines 3243–3278); §A Agent Instructions (lines 3842–3910)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/skb/calibrator.py` — SKB-04 deterministic calibrator (v2 prior)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/skb/edge_detection.py` — SKB-14 edge detection (v2 prior)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/skb/colour_sampler.py` — SKB-07 region colour sampler (v2 prior)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/commands.py` — `Command` union; add `TraceImageCmd` here
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine.py` — command dispatch; add handler here
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/api/registry.py` — tool descriptor registry (API-V3-01 contract)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/cli/cli.mjs` — CLI entry point; register `bim-ai trace` here
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/jobs/queue.py` — Jobs queue (JOB-V3-01); large traces run as jobs
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/jobs/types.py` — Job type definitions

---

## Verbatim IMG-V3-01 tracker entry (§6 T9, line 3243)

**IMG-V3-01 — Image-input → structured-layout tool (deterministic CV)**

_Source: R-A §8 (AI tracing image → vector); R-C §2 + §4 (AutoLayout multi-modal input). Reframed entirely from "agent traces sketch via multimodal AI" to "**deterministic CV** tool that produces a structured layout an external agent can read." The wire-format JSON is the contract; the CV implementation is swappable._

**Status.** `now` for the deterministic CV path. `vision` for AI-swappable upgrades.
**Scope.** A deterministic CV tool — line detection, polygon recovery, optional OCR for dimensions — that takes an image and returns a structured layout JSON. **No AI runs inside the binary.** Pipeline:

1. **Preprocessing.** SKB-04 calibrator + SKB-14 edge detection + SKB-07 region colour sampler (v2 primitives).
2. **Polygon recovery.** Hough line detection → contour fitting → room-region segmentation. Pure CV.
3. **Dimension OCR (optional).** Tesseract on labelled strings; rejected gracefully if illegible.
4. **Layout JSON emission.** Returns a `StructuredLayout` round-trippable into TKN-V3-01's `EnvelopeToken[]`.

The output JSON is the wire format an external agent reads. The agent decides how to interpret it — refine with its own vision, call CMD-V3-01 directly, combine with a brief. The CV is the **floor**: a deterministic baseline that always works. AI augmentations are external and optional.

**Data model.** New `StructuredLayout` type designed to be both human-debuggable and TKN-V3-01-compatible:
```ts
type StructuredLayout = {
  schemaVersion: 'img-v3.0';
  imageMetadata: { widthPx: number; heightPx: number; calibrationMmPerPx?: number };
  rooms: Array<{
    id: string;
    polygonMm: Array<{ x: number; y: number }>;
    detectedTypeKey?: string;                // CV-detected room type from glyph / colour
    detectedAreaMm2?: number;
  }>;
  walls: Array<{ id: string; aMm: { x: number; y: number }; bMm: { x: number; y: number }; thicknessMm?: number }>;
  openings: Array<{ id: string; hostWallId: string; tAlongWall: number; widthMm?: number; kindHint?: 'door' | 'window' }>;
  ocrLabels: Array<{ text: string; bboxMm: { x: number; y: number; w: number; h: number }; confidence: number }>;
  advisories: Advisory[];                    // 'low_contrast_image', 'no_dimensions_detected', etc.
};
```
**Engine.** New CLI: `bim-ai trace --image <path> [--brief <path>] [--archetype-hint <key>] -o layout.json`. Runs SKB-04 + SKB-14 + SKB-07 (deterministic) + polygon recovery + optional OCR. Output: `(StructuredLayout, advisories)`. Registered as a tool descriptor per API-V3-01. The brief is passed through as metadata only — the deterministic CV does not interpret it; agents that read the brief do their own thing with it.
**UI.** Drop zone on canvas; "Trace as new model" enqueues a job. On completion, side-by-side preview of the source image + a SVG render of the structured layout. The user can manually correct the layout (edit walls, re-label rooms) before handing off to an agent for downstream interpretation.
**Acceptance.** (a) Drop `target-house-vis-colored.png`; trace completes in <10 s wall-clock on a single CPU; output `StructuredLayout` JSON contains ≥10 rooms and ≥40 walls. (b) Same image dropped twice → byte-identical JSON output (deterministic). (c) `bim-ai trace --image low-contrast.png` returns a layout with `low_contrast_image` advisory + degraded-but-sane polygons. (d) An external agent harness reads the layout JSON, calls CMD-V3-01 to seed the model, kernel reconstructs a valid model with green soundness pack.
**Effort.** L — 3 weeks (deterministic CV is non-trivial; OCR integration adds a week).
**Cross-theme references.** **API-V3-01 (registered tool).** **TKN-V3-01 (output JSON is round-trippable into envelope tokens).** **T6 (image-side primitives — IMG-V3-01 is the bridge from T6 to the kernel).** **JOB-V3-01 (large traces run as jobs).** **v2 SKB-04, SKB-07, SKB-14 (deterministic priors).**

---

## Setup

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
git checkout -b feat/v3-img-v3-01-image-to-layout
python -c "from bim_ai.skb.calibrator import calibrate; print('skb ok')"
uv sync && pnpm install
```

---

## Implementation rules (verbatim from §A)

- **Honour API-V3-01.** Every kernel command you add must have CLI + REST/RPC + JSON schema + assumption-log support. Add tests at the agent-callable layer (`tests/cli/`, `tests/api/`).
- **No scope creep.** Do not silently add features not in the tracker. v3 is a 4–6 month commitment — scope creep is the largest risk. If a requirement would expand §L Non-Goals, stop and escalate.
- **Design-pillar adherence.** Do not skim §3 Design Pillars or the anti-pattern catalog (A1–A10). A WP that violates D1 (direct manipulation first) or A9 (always-visible Properties panel) ships incomplete.
- **No hex literals in `packages/web/src/**/*.tsx`.** Use CSS token variables only. The ESLint rule (R-G §2.5 phase 3) will fail builds that introduce hex literals.
- **No AI runs inside the binary.** The `bim-ai trace` CLI is pure deterministic CV. The wire-format JSON is the contract; AI augmentations are external and optional.
- **Determinism is a hard requirement.** Same image in → byte-identical JSON out. Every intermediate CV step must be seeded / stateless. CI will call trace twice on the same input and diff the outputs.
- **Use JOB-V3-01 for large images.** Any trace that takes >2 s wall-clock must be dispatched through the jobs queue and cancellable on user request.

---

## Implementation tasks

### 1. New module `app/bim_ai/img/`

Create the package:

```
app/bim_ai/img/__init__.py
app/bim_ai/img/types.py        # StructuredLayout + Advisory Pydantic models
app/bim_ai/img/pipeline.py     # trace() entry point
app/bim_ai/img/polygon.py      # Hough line → contour → room segmentation
app/bim_ai/img/ocr.py          # Tesseract wrapper (graceful fallback)
```

`types.py` — define `StructuredLayout`, `RoomRegion`, `WallSegment`, `OpeningHint`, `OcrLabel`, `Advisory` as Pydantic models matching the data model above. `schemaVersion` is a `Literal['img-v3.0']` field.

`pipeline.py` — `trace(image_path: str, *, archetype_hint: str | None = None, brief_path: str | None = None) -> StructuredLayout`. Calls SKB-04 calibrator → SKB-14 edge detection → SKB-07 colour sampler → `polygon.recover_rooms()` → `ocr.extract_labels()`. Brief is attached as `imageMetadata.briefPath` string only — not interpreted. All CV steps must be deterministic (no random seeds, no probabilistic models).

`polygon.py` — `recover_rooms(edges, calibration) -> tuple[list[RoomRegion], list[WallSegment], list[OpeningHint]]`. Implements Hough line detection → contour fitting → room segmentation. Use `opencv-python` (already in constraints; no new deps without asking).

`ocr.py` — `extract_labels(image, calibration) -> list[OcrLabel]`. Wraps `pytesseract`; on `ImportError` or Tesseract-not-installed, returns `[]` and appends `Advisory(code='tesseract_unavailable')`. Never raises.

### 2. CLI command (`packages/cli/cli.mjs`)

Register `bim-ai trace`:

```
bim-ai trace --image <path> [--brief <path>] [--archetype-hint <key>] [-o <output.json>]
```

- Calls `POST /api/v3/trace` (for large images: enqueues a job via JOB-V3-01 queue; for small images: runs inline).
- Prints `StructuredLayout` JSON to stdout (or writes to `-o` path).
- Exit code 0 on success; exit code 1 if the layout contains a fatal advisory (`no_walls_detected`).

### 3. API tool descriptor (`app/bim_ai/api/registry.py`)

Register `img_trace` tool per API-V3-01 contract: `{ name, description, inputSchema (JSON Schema for TraceImageCmd), outputSchema (JSON Schema for StructuredLayout) }`.

### 4. REST route (`app/bim_ai/routes_api.py` or new `routes_trace.py`)

`POST /api/v3/trace` — accepts `multipart/form-data` with `image` file + optional `brief` file + optional `archetypeHint` string. For images >2 MB, enqueues a `TraceJob` via the jobs queue and returns `{ jobId }`. For images ≤2 MB, runs inline and returns `StructuredLayout` JSON directly.

### 5. `TraceImageCmd` (`app/bim_ai/commands.py`)

```python
class TraceImageCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["TraceImage"] = "TraceImage"
    image_b64: str = Field(alias="imageB64")          # base64-encoded image bytes
    archetype_hint: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="archetypeHint")
    brief_text: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="briefText")
    assumptions: list = Field(default_factory=list)   # API-V3-01 assumption log
```

Add to the `Command` union. Engine handler calls `img.pipeline.trace()` and returns the `StructuredLayout` as the command result (no kernel mutation — trace is a read-only analysis tool).

### 6. TypeScript types (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)

```ts
export type Advisory = {
  code: string;
  message?: string;
};

export type StructuredLayout = {
  schemaVersion: 'img-v3.0';
  imageMetadata: { widthPx: number; heightPx: number; calibrationMmPerPx?: number };
  rooms: Array<{
    id: string;
    polygonMm: Array<{ x: number; y: number }>;
    detectedTypeKey?: string;
    detectedAreaMm2?: number;
  }>;
  walls: Array<{ id: string; aMm: { x: number; y: number }; bMm: { x: number; y: number }; thicknessMm?: number }>;
  openings: Array<{ id: string; hostWallId: string; tAlongWall: number; widthMm?: number; kindHint?: 'door' | 'window' }>;
  ocrLabels: Array<{ text: string; bboxMm: { x: number; y: number; w: number; h: number }; confidence: number }>;
  advisories: Advisory[];
};
```

### 7. UI: image drop zone (`packages/web/src/plan/ImageTraceDropZone.tsx`)

- Accepts `.png`, `.jpg`, `.jpeg`, `.pdf` via drag-and-drop or file picker.
- On drop: `POST /api/v3/trace` with `multipart/form-data`. If response is `{ jobId }`, tracks the job via JOB-V3-01; if response is `StructuredLayout`, renders immediately.
- On completion: side-by-side preview — source image on the left, SVG render of detected walls/rooms on the right.
- SVG render uses `--draft-lw-cut-major` for walls and `--color-surface-strong` for room fills at 0.15 opacity.
- No hex literals. All colours via CSS token variables.

### 8. Tests (`app/tests/test_img_v3_01.py`)

Cover:

- `trace()` on a synthetic PNG with a simple rectangle → `StructuredLayout` with ≥1 room and ≥4 walls.
- `trace()` called twice on the same file → byte-identical JSON output (determinism gate).
- Low-contrast image (flat grey PNG) → layout contains `low_contrast_image` advisory; polygons list is non-empty (degraded-but-sane).
- `ocr.extract_labels()` when Tesseract unavailable → returns `[]` + `tesseract_unavailable` advisory; does not raise.
- `TraceImageCmd` dispatched through engine → returns a dict with `schemaVersion == 'img-v3.0'`.
- `TraceImageCmd` with no `assumptions` field → still accepted (assumption log is optional for analysis-only tools).

python -m pytest tests/test_img_v3_01.py -v
python -m pytest tests/ -x -q
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai

1	"""SKB-04 — sketch-to-dimensions calibrator.
2	
3	The agent rarely gets every dimension from the sketch; usually 2-3 are
4	called out, and the rest is proportional. This module takes a list of
5	**anchors** (each pinning a pixel-distance to a real-world mm distance),
6	computes a pixel-to-mm scale, and exposes a query API the agent uses
7	when it needs a derived measurement.
8	
9	No model. Pure math. The agent is still the brain — it provides anchors,
10	the calibrator gives back consistent numbers.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	from collections.abc import Iterable, Sequence
16	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
17	
18	
19	@dataclass(frozen=True)
20	class Anchor:
21	    """One dimension call-out: a pixel distance corresponds to a real
22	    measurement in mm. `axis` is `'x'` or `'y'` to disambiguate
23	    perspective-distorted sketches.
24	    """
25	
26	    label: str
27	    pixels: float
28	    millimeters: float
29	    axis: str = "any"   # 'x' | 'y' | 'any'
30	
31	
32	@dataclass(frozen=True)
33	class CalibrationResult:
34	    """The agreed scale with provenance."""
35	
36	    scale_mm_per_px: float
37	    axis: str               # 'x', 'y', or 'any' when isotropic
38	    anchor_count: int
39	    residual_pct: float     # max % disagreement between anchors at the chosen scale
40	    notes: str = ""
41	
42	    def pixels_to_mm(self, pixels: float) -> float:
43	        return pixels * self.scale_mm_per_px
44	
45	    def mm_to_pixels(self, mm: float) -> float:
46	        if self.scale_mm_per_px == 0:
47	            return 0.0
48	        return mm / self.scale_mm_per_px
49	
50	
51	def calibrate(anchors: Sequence[Anchor]) -> CalibrationResult:
52	    """Compute a single scale from one or more anchors.
53	
54	    With a single anchor: scale = mm / pixels exactly.
55	    With multiple: median scale; residual = max abs % deviation from median.
56	    Raises ValueError on no anchors or zero-pixel anchors.
57	    """
58	    if not anchors:
59	        raise ValueError("calibrate(): need at least one anchor")
60	    for a in anchors:
61	        if a.pixels <= 0:
62	            raise ValueError(f"anchor {a.label!r}: pixels must be > 0 (got {a.pixels})")
63	        if a.millimeters <= 0:
64	            raise ValueError(f"anchor {a.label!r}: millimeters must be > 0 (got {a.millimeters})")
65	
66	    scales = sorted(a.millimeters / a.pixels for a in anchors)
67	    n = len(scales)
68	    median = scales[n // 2] if n % 2 == 1 else 0.5 * (scales[n // 2 - 1] + scales[n // 2])
69	
70	    if n == 1:
71	        residual = 0.0
72	        notes = "single anchor: exact"
73	    else:
74	        residual = 100.0 * max(abs(s - median) for s in scales) / median
75	        notes = f"{n} anchors; max residual {residual:.2f}%"
76	
77	    # Determine axis: isotropic if all anchors share an axis or all are 'any'.
78	    axes = {a.axis for a in anchors}
79	    if axes == {"any"} or len(axes) > 1:
80	        out_axis = "any"
81	    else:
82	        out_axis = next(iter(axes))
83	
84	    return CalibrationResult(
85	        scale_mm_per_px=median,
86	        axis=out_axis,
87	        anchor_count=n,
88	        residual_pct=residual,
89	        notes=notes,
90	    )
91	
92	
93	@dataclass(frozen=True)
94	class CalibratedSketch:
95	    """A sketch whose pixel-space has been calibrated via one or more
96	    anchors. Subsequent agent queries hit the cached `Calibration`."""
97	
98	    image_path: str | None
99	    calibration: CalibrationResult
100	    anchors: list[Anchor] = field(default_factory=list)
101	
102	    def measure_pixels(self, pixels: float) -> float:
103	        """Return mm corresponding to a pixel distance."""
104	        return self.calibration.pixels_to_mm(pixels)
105	
106	    def measure_segment(self, p1: tuple[float, float], p2: tuple[float, float]) -> float:
107	        """mm length of the pixel segment p1 → p2."""
108	        dx, dy = p2[0] - p1[0], p2[1] - p1[1]
109	        d_px = (dx * dx + dy * dy) ** 0.5
110	        return self.measure_pixels(d_px)
111	
112	    def position_to_mm(self, p: tuple[float, float], origin_px: tuple[float, float] = (0, 0)) -> tuple[float, float]:
113	        """Convert a pixel position to a mm-space position relative to
114	        the given pixel origin (default = pixel (0, 0))."""
115	        return (
116	            self.calibration.pixels_to_mm(p[0] - origin_px[0]),
117	            self.calibration.pixels_to_mm(p[1] - origin_px[1]),
118	        )
119	
120	
121	def make_calibrated_sketch(
122	    anchors: Iterable[Anchor],
123	    image_path: str | None = None,
124	) -> CalibratedSketch:
125	    """Convenience: calibrate + bundle the anchors into a CalibratedSketch."""
126	    anchor_list = list(anchors)
127	    cal = calibrate(anchor_list)
128	    return CalibratedSketch(image_path=image_path, calibration=cal, anchors=anchor_list)
129

"""SKB-14 — sketch edge-detection helper (classical CV).

Deterministic vision aid: runs Canny edge-detection on a sketch / colour
study and writes an edges-only PNG. Massing outlines pop out more crisply
than the original drawing.

The agent reads the edges PNG with its own vision and traces polygons.
Pure OpenCV (or scikit-image fallback). No model.

Avoids the heavy OpenCV dep at import-time — the function imports cv2
lazily so unrelated tests don't pay the cost.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class EdgeDetectionResult:
    input_path: str
    output_path: str
    canny_low: int
    canny_high: int
    blur_sigma: float
    edge_pixel_count: int
    image_size: tuple[int, int]   # (width, height) in pixels


def detect_edges(
    input_path: str | Path,
    output_path: str | Path,
    canny_low: int = 50,
    canny_high: int = 150,
    blur_sigma: float = 1.4,
) -> EdgeDetectionResult:
    """Run Canny edge detection and write an edges-only PNG.

    `canny_low` / `canny_high` are the hysteresis thresholds (0-255).
    `blur_sigma` is the Gaussian blur sigma applied before edge detection
    to suppress sketch noise; pass 0 to skip blur.

    Returns metadata about the run that callers can log to evidence.
    """
    in_path = Path(input_path)
    out_path = Path(output_path)
    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    try:
        import cv2  # type: ignore[import-not-found]

        img = cv2.imread(str(in_path), cv2.IMREAD_GRAYSCALE)
        if img is None:
            raise FileNotFoundError(f"could not read image: {in_path}")
        if blur_sigma > 0:
            ksize = max(3, int(round(blur_sigma * 3)) | 1)
            img = cv2.GaussianBlur(img, (ksize, ksize), blur_sigma)
        edges = cv2.Canny(img, canny_low, canny_high)
        cv2.imwrite(str(out_path), edges)
        edge_count = int((edges > 0).sum())
        h, w = edges.shape
        return EdgeDetectionResult(
            input_path=str(in_path),
            output_path=str(out_path),
            canny_low=canny_low,
            canny_high=canny_high,
            blur_sigma=blur_sigma,
            edge_pixel_count=edge_count,
            image_size=(int(w), int(h)),
        )
    except ImportError:
        # scikit-image fallback
        from skimage import feature, filters, img_as_ubyte, io

        img = io.imread(str(in_path), as_gray=True)
        if blur_sigma > 0:
            img = filters.gaussian(img, sigma=blur_sigma)
        edges = feature.canny(
            img,
            sigma=blur_sigma if blur_sigma > 0 else 1.0,
            low_threshold=canny_low / 255.0,
            high_threshold=canny_high / 255.0,
        )
        edges_u8 = img_as_ubyte(edges)
        io.imsave(str(out_path), edges_u8)
        edge_count = int(edges.sum())
        h, w = edges.shape
        return EdgeDetectionResult(
            input_path=str(in_path),
            output_path=str(out_path),
            canny_low=canny_low,
            canny_high=canny_high,
            blur_sigma=blur_sigma,
            edge_pixel_count=edge_count,
            image_size=(int(w), int(h)),
        )


def edge_density(result: EdgeDetectionResult) -> float:
    """Edge pixels / total pixels — quick check that detection didn't
    return all-black (failed) or all-white (over-saturated)."""
    w, h = result.image_size
    if w == 0 or h == 0:
        return 0.0
    return result.edge_pixel_count / float(w * h)

1	"""SKB-07 — region colour sampler.
2	
3	Deterministic vision aid: takes a sketch / colour-study PNG and a
4	rectangular region (or polygon), samples the dominant RGB, and returns
5	the top-K nearest matches from the MAT-01 catalog.
6	
7	No model. No vision LLM. Pure NumPy / Pillow + sRGB Euclidean distance
8	in CIE Lab space. The agent is still the brain; this is a calibrated
9	ruler that maps "what colour is this facade in the colour study?" to
10	"the agent should set materialKey: 'cladding_beige_grey'".
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	from collections.abc import Sequence
16	from dataclasses import dataclass
17	from pathlib import Path
18	
19	
20	@dataclass(frozen=True)
21	class CatalogColourEntry:
22	    """A material catalog entry mapped to its sRGB hex."""
23	
24	    material_key: str
25	    base_color_hex: str
26	    display_name: str
27	
28	
29	@dataclass(frozen=True)
30	class ColourMatch:
31	    material_key: str
32	    display_name: str
33	    catalog_hex: str
34	    sample_hex: str
35	    distance: float
36	
37	
38	def _hex_to_rgb(hex_str: str) -> tuple[int, int, int]:
39	    h = hex_str.lstrip("#")
40	    if len(h) == 3:
41	        h = "".join(c * 2 for c in h)
42	    return int(h[0:2], 16), int(h[2:4], 16), int(h[4:6], 16)
43	
44	
45	def _rgb_to_hex(rgb: tuple[int, int, int]) -> str:
46	    r, g, b = rgb

"""SKB-07 — region colour sampler.

Deterministic vision aid: takes a sketch / colour-study PNG and a
rectangular region (or polygon), samples the dominant RGB, and returns
the top-K nearest matches from the MAT-01 catalog.

No model. No vision LLM. Pure NumPy / Pillow + sRGB Euclidean distance
in CIE Lab space. The agent is still the brain; this is a calibrated
ruler that maps "what colour is this facade in the colour study?" to
"the agent should set materialKey: 'cladding_beige_grey'".
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Sequence
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CatalogColourEntry:
    """A material catalog entry mapped to its sRGB hex."""

    material_key: str
    base_color_hex: str
    display_name: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ColourMatch:
    material_key: str
    display_name: str
    catalog_hex: str
    sample_hex: str
    distance: float


def _hex_to_rgb(hex_str: str) -> tuple[int, int, int]:
    h = hex_str.lstrip("#")
    if len(h) == 3:
        h = "".join(c * 2 for c in h)
    return int(h[0:2], 16), int(h[2:4], 16), int(h[4:6], 16)


def _rgb_to_hex(rgb: tuple[int, int, int]) -> str:
    r, g, b = rgb
    return f"#{r:02x}{g:02x}{b:02x}"


def _srgb_to_linear(c: float) -> float:
    c /= 255.0
    return c / 12.92 if c <= 0.04045 else ((c + 0.055) / 1.055) ** 2.4


def _rgb_to_xyz(rgb: tuple[int, int, int]) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
    r, g, b = (_srgb_to_linear(v) for v in rgb)
    # sRGB D65
    x = 0.4124564 * r + 0.3575761 * g + 0.1804375 * b
    y = 0.2126729 * r + 0.7151522 * g + 0.0721750 * b
    z = 0.0193339 * r + 0.1191920 * g + 0.9503041 * b
    return x, y, z


def _xyz_to_lab(xyz: tuple[float, float, float]) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
    # D65 reference white
    xn, yn, zn = 0.95047, 1.0, 1.08883
    x, y, z = xyz
    fx, fy, fz = (_pivot(v / ref) for v, ref in zip((x, y, z), (xn, yn, zn), strict=False))
    L = 116 * fy - 16
    a = 500 * (fx - fy)
    b = 200 * (fy - fz)
    return L, a, b


def _pivot(t: float) -> float:
    return t ** (1.0 / 3.0) if t > (216 / 24389) else (24389 / 27 * t + 16) / 116


def _delta_e(a: tuple[int, int, int], b: tuple[int, int, int]) -> float:
    """CIE76 Delta-E in Lab space (sufficient for swatch matching)."""
    la, aa, ba = _xyz_to_lab(_rgb_to_xyz(a))
    lb, ab, bb = _xyz_to_lab(_rgb_to_xyz(b))
    return ((la - lb) ** 2 + (aa - ab) ** 2 + (ba - bb) ** 2) ** 0.5


def sample_region_mean_rgb(
    image_path: str | Path,
    region_xyxy: tuple[int, int, int, int],
) -> tuple[int, int, int]:
    """Mean sRGB of a rectangular region of the image. Coordinates are
    pixel `(x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max)` with origin top-left.

    Uses Pillow + numpy. Returns ints in [0, 255].
    """
    import numpy as np
    from PIL import Image

    img = Image.open(image_path).convert("RGB")
    x0, y0, x1, y1 = region_xyxy
    crop = img.crop((x0, y0, x1, y1))
    arr = np.asarray(crop, dtype=np.float32)
    if arr.size == 0:
        raise ValueError(f"empty region {region_xyxy} in {image_path}")
    mean = arr.reshape(-1, 3).mean(axis=0)
    return tuple(int(round(v)) for v in mean)  # type: ignore[return-value]


def nearest_matches(
    sample_rgb: tuple[int, int, int],
    catalog: Sequence[CatalogColourEntry],
    top_k: int = 3,
) -> list[ColourMatch]:
    """Return the `top_k` nearest catalog matches for a sample colour
    using CIE Lab Delta-E. Smallest distance is the closest match.
    """
    rows: list[ColourMatch] = []
    for entry in catalog:
        cat_rgb = _hex_to_rgb(entry.base_color_hex)
        d = _delta_e(sample_rgb, cat_rgb)
        rows.append(
            ColourMatch(
                material_key=entry.material_key,
                display_name=entry.display_name,
                catalog_hex=entry.base_color_hex,
                sample_hex=_rgb_to_hex(sample_rgb),
                distance=d,
            )
        )
    rows.sort(key=lambda r: r.distance)
    return rows[:top_k]


# Pre-baked catalog mirroring MAT-01 keys with hex base colors. Kept here
# (rather than importing from web/) so this module is purely Python-side.
MAT01_COLOUR_CATALOG: list[CatalogColourEntry] = [
    CatalogColourEntry("timber_cladding", "#8b6340", "Timber cladding (orange-brown)"),
    CatalogColourEntry("white_cladding", "#f4f4f0", "White cladding"),
    CatalogColourEntry("white_render", "#f4f4f0", "White render"),
    CatalogColourEntry("cladding_beige_grey", "#c4b59a", "Beige-grey cladding"),
    CatalogColourEntry("cladding_warm_wood", "#a87a44", "Warm wood cladding"),
    CatalogColourEntry("cladding_dark_grey", "#3a3d3f", "Dark-grey cladding"),
    CatalogColourEntry("render_light_grey", "#cfd0cd", "Light-grey render"),
    CatalogColourEntry("render_beige", "#d8c8a8", "Beige render"),
    CatalogColourEntry("render_terracotta", "#a85432", "Terracotta render"),
    CatalogColourEntry("aluminium_dark_grey", "#3d4042", "Dark-grey aluminium"),
    CatalogColourEntry("aluminium_natural", "#a8acaf", "Natural aluminium"),
    CatalogColourEntry("aluminium_black", "#1c1d1e", "Black aluminium"),
    CatalogColourEntry("brick_red", "#8a3a26", "Red brick"),
    CatalogColourEntry("brick_yellow", "#c5a857", "Yellow brick"),
    CatalogColourEntry("brick_grey", "#7a7873", "Grey brick"),
    CatalogColourEntry("stone_limestone", "#d8d0bc", "Limestone"),
    CatalogColourEntry("stone_slate", "#3e3a35", "Slate"),
    CatalogColourEntry("stone_sandstone", "#b89968", "Sandstone"),
    CatalogColourEntry("concrete_smooth", "#9c9a94", "Smooth concrete"),
    CatalogColourEntry("metal_standing_seam_dark_grey", "#3a3d3f", "Standing-seam metal — dark grey"),
    CatalogColourEntry("metal_standing_seam_zinc", "#7a7d80", "Standing-seam metal — zinc"),
    CatalogColourEntry("metal_standing_seam_copper", "#b86b3c", "Standing-seam metal — copper"),
    CatalogColourEntry("glass_clear", "#e6efef", "Clear glass"),
]


def sample_and_match(
    image_path: str | Path,
    region_xyxy: tuple[int, int, int, int],
    top_k: int = 3,
    catalog: Sequence[CatalogColourEntry] | None = None,
) -> list[ColourMatch]:
    """One-shot helper: sample mean RGB of a region, return top-K matches."""
    cat = catalog if catalog is not None else MAT01_COLOUR_CATALOG
    rgb = sample_region_mean_rgb(image_path, region_xyxy)
    return nearest_matches(rgb, cat, top_k=top_k)

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Annotated, Any, Literal

from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, model_validator

from bim_ai.elements import (
    BalusterPattern,
    CameraMm,
    ClipRect,
    ConstraintRefRow,
    ConstraintRule,
    CurtainPanelOverride,
    DormerPositionOnRoof,
    DormerRoofKind,
    DxfLineworkPrim,
    EvidenceRef,
    HandrailSupport,
    PhaseFilter,
    PlanCategoryGraphicRow,
    PlanTagBadgeStyle,
    PlanTagTarget,
    RoomColorSchemeRow,
    SiteContextObjectRow,
    StairLanding,
    StairRun,
    StairTreadLine,
    SweepPathPoint,
    SweepProfilePlane,
    SweepProfilePoint,
    Text3dFontFamily,
    Vec2Mm,
    Vec3Mm,
    WallRecessZone,
    WallTypeLayer,
)
from bim_ai.roof_geometry import RoofGeometryMode


class CreateLevelCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createLevel"] = "createLevel"
    id: str | None = None
    name: str = "Level"
    elevation_mm: float = Field(alias="elevationMm", default=0)
    datum_kind: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="datumKind")
    parent_level_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="parentLevelId")
    offset_from_parent_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="offsetFromParentMm")
    # VIE-05: when True (default), the engine also creates a "<name> — Plan"
    # plan_view referencing the new level so the common flow needs no follow-up.
    also_create_plan_view: bool = Field(default=True, alias="alsoCreatePlanView")
    plan_view_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="planViewId")


class WallStackComponentCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    wall_type_id: str = Field(alias="wallTypeId")
    height_mm: float = Field(alias="heightMm", gt=0)


class CreateWallCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["createWall"] = "createWall"
    id: str | None = None
    name: str = "Wall"
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    start: Vec2Mm
    end: Vec2Mm
    thickness_mm: float = Field(alias="thicknessMm", default=200)
    height_mm: float = Field(alias="heightMm", default=2800)
    wall_type_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="wallTypeId")
    base_constraint_level_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="baseConstraintLevelId")
    top_constraint_level_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="topConstraintLevelId")
    base_constraint_offset_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="baseConstraintOffsetMm")
    top_constraint_offset_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="topConstraintOffsetMm")
    insulation_extension_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="insulationExtensionMm")
    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")
    is_curtain_wall: bool = Field(default=False, alias="isCurtainWall")
    stack_components: list[WallStackComponentCmd] = Field(
        default_factory=list, alias="stackComponents"
    )
    lean_mm: Vec2Mm | None = Field(default=None, alias="leanMm")
    taper_ratio: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="taperRatio")


class SetWallStackCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["setWallStack"] = "setWallStack"
    wall_id: str = Field(alias="wallId")
    components: list[WallStackComponentCmd] = Field(default_factory=list)


class SetWallLeanTaperCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["setWallLeanTaper"] = "setWallLeanTaper"
    wall_id: str = Field(alias="wallId")
    lean_mm: Vec2Mm | None = Field(default=None, alias="leanMm")
    taper_ratio: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="taperRatio")

1	"""API-V3-01 — Tool registry.
2	
3	Every kernel verb registers a ToolDescriptor here at boot.  The registry is
4	in-memory and populated by calling `register()` at module import time (or from
5	each theme WP's own module when it lands).
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	from dataclasses import dataclass
11	from typing import Any, Literal
12	
13	
14	@dataclass(frozen=True)
15	class RestEndpoint:
16	    method: Literal["GET", "POST"]
17	    path: str
18	
19	
20	@dataclass(frozen=True)
21	class ExitCode:
22	    code: int
23	    meaning: str
24	
25	
26	@dataclass
27	class ToolDescriptor:
28	    name: str
29	    category: Literal["query", "mutation", "transform", "job", "introspection"]
30	    inputSchema: dict[str, Any]
31	    outputSchema: dict[str, Any]
32	    exitCodes: dict[str, ExitCode]

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
from collections import defaultdict
from collections.abc import Awaitable, Callable
from datetime import UTC, datetime

from .types import Job, JobOutputs, JobStatus


class JobQueue:
    """Single-process in-memory job queue for v3.0.

    Pluggable for Redis/Celery in v3.1 — callers interact via this interface only.
    # TODO(v3.1): persist to DB
    """

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._jobs: dict[str, Job] = {}
        self._model_index: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
        self._subscribers: list[Callable[[Job], Awaitable[None]]] = []
        self._lock = asyncio.Lock()

    async def submit(self, job: Job) -> Job:
        async with self._lock:
            self._jobs[job.id] = job
            self._model_index[job.model_id].append(job.id)
        await self._notify(job)
        return job

    async def update_status(
        self,
        job_id: str,
        status: JobStatus,
        *,
        error_message: str | None = None,
        outputs: JobOutputs | None = None,
    ) -> Job:
        async with self._lock:
            job = self._jobs[job_id]
            now = datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()
            updates: dict = {"status": status}
            if status == "running":
                updates["started_at"] = now
            elif status in ("done", "errored", "cancelled"):
                updates["completed_at"] = now
            if error_message is not None:
                updates["error_message"] = error_message
            if outputs is not None:
                updates["outputs"] = outputs
            updated = job.model_copy(update=updates)
            self._jobs[job_id] = updated
        await self._notify(updated)
        return updated

    def get(self, job_id: str) -> Job | None:
        return self._jobs.get(job_id)

    def list_for_model(self, model_id: str) -> list[Job]:
        ids = self._model_index.get(model_id, [])
        return [self._jobs[jid] for jid in ids if jid in self._jobs]

    def subscribe(self, callback: Callable[[Job], Awaitable[None]]) -> None:
        self._subscribers.append(callback)

    async def _notify(self, job: Job) -> None:
        for sub in self._subscribers:
            try:
                await sub(job)
            except Exception:
                pass  # best-effort


_queue: JobQueue | None = None


def get_queue() -> JobQueue:
    global _queue
    if _queue is None:
        _queue = JobQueue()
    return _queue

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from typing import Any, Literal

from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field

JobKind = Literal[
    "csg_solve",
    "ifc_export",
    "dxf_import",
    "gltf_export",
    "sketch_trace",
    "render_still",
    "render_video",
    "agent_call",
]

JobStatus = Literal["queued", "running", "done", "errored", "cancelled"]


class JobOutputs(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)
    primary_asset_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="primaryAssetId")
    secondary_asset_ids: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="secondaryAssetIds")


class JobCostEstimate(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)
    credits: float


class Job(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)
    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    model_id: str = Field(alias="modelId")
    kind: JobKind
    status: JobStatus = "queued"
    inputs: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
    outputs: JobOutputs | None = None
    created_at: str = Field(alias="createdAt")
    started_at: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="startedAt")
    completed_at: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="completedAt")
    error_message: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="errorMessage")
    cost_estimate: JobCostEstimate | None = Field(default=None, alias="costEstimate")
    parent_job_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="parentJobId")


class CreateJobRequest(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)
    kind: JobKind
    inputs: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)

129	async def resolve_caller_role(
130	    session: AsyncSession, model_id: str | UUID, user_id: str
131	) -> str:
132	    """Return the caller's role for model_id. Defaults to 'admin' when no record exists."""
133	    res = await session.execute(
134	        select(RoleAssignmentRecord).where(
135	            RoleAssignmentRecord.model_id == str(model_id),
136	            RoleAssignmentRecord.subject_kind == "user",
137	            RoleAssignmentRecord.subject_id == user_id,
138	        )
139	    )
140	    record = res.scalars().first()
141	    return record.role if record is not None else "admin"
142	
143	
144	async def _resolve_token_role(
145	    session: AsyncSession, model_id_str: str, token: str
146	) -> str:
147	    """Resolve a public-link token to a role; raises 403 if invalid or expired."""
148	    now_ms = int(time.time() * 1000)
149	    res = await session.execute(
150	        select(RoleAssignmentRecord).where(
151	            RoleAssignmentRecord.model_id == model_id_str,
152	            RoleAssignmentRecord.subject_kind == "public-link",
153	            RoleAssignmentRecord.subject_id == token,
154	        )
155	    )
156	    record = res.scalars().first()
157	    if record is None:
158	        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Invalid public-link token")
159	    if record.expires_at is not None and record.expires_at < now_ms:
160	        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Public-link token has expired")
161	    return record.role

219	@api_router.post("/projects/{project_id}/models")
220	async def create_empty_model(
221	    project_id: UUID,
222	    body: CreateEmptyModelBody,
223	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
224	) -> dict[str, Any]:
225	    proj = await session.get(ProjectRecord, project_id)
226	    if proj is None:
227	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Project not found")
228	    mid = uuid4()
229	
230	    if body.template_id:
231	        if not template_exists(body.template_id):
232	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Template '{body.template_id}' not found")
233	        try:
234	            seed_doc = load_template_snapshot(body.template_id)
235	        except (FileNotFoundError, LookupError) as exc:
236	            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(exc)) from exc
237	        # Reset revision so the new model starts at 1 regardless of template state.
238	        seed_doc.revision = 1
239	    else:
240	        seed_doc = Document(revision=1, elements={})  # type: ignore[arg-type]
241	
242	    # KRN-06: every new model has the singleton internal_origin from inception.
243	    ensure_internal_origin(seed_doc)
244	    ensure_sun_settings(seed_doc)
245	    wire = document_to_wire(seed_doc)
246	    row = ModelRecord(
247	        id=mid,
248	        project_id=project_id,
249	        slug=body.slug,
250	        revision=seed_doc.revision,
251	        document=wire,
252	    )
253	    session.add(row)
254	    try:
255	        await session.commit()
256	    except IntegrityError:
257	        await session.rollback()
258	        raise HTTPException(
259	            status_code=409,
260	            detail="Model slug already exists for this project",
261	        ) from None
262	    return {
263	        "id": str(mid),
264	        "projectId": str(project_id),
265	        "slug": body.slug,
266	        "revision": row.revision,
267	        "templateId": body.template_id,
268	    }

288	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
289	# Model read routes
290	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
291	
292	
293	@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/snapshot")
294	async def snapshot(
295	    model_id: UUID,
296	    expandLinks: bool = False,  # noqa: N803 — wire-format alias
297	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
298	) -> dict[str, Any]:
299	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
300	    if row is None:
301	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
302	    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
303	    # KRN-06: backfill internal_origin for legacy models that pre-date this WP.
304	    # Read-only — we don't persist; the next command commit will pick it up.
305	    ensure_internal_origin(doc)
306	    ensure_sun_settings(doc)
307	    elements_wire = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc.elements.items()}
308	    if expandLinks:
309	        # FED-01: inline every linked source's elements with provenance markers
310	        # so renderers can ghost them. Default snapshot omits these to keep the
311	        # payload small.
312	        elements_wire = await _expand_host_links(session, doc, elements_wire)
313	    link_source_revisions = await _resolve_link_source_revisions(session, doc)
314	    out: dict[str, Any] = {
315	        "modelId": str(row.id),
316	        "revision": doc.revision,
317	        "elements": elements_wire,
318	        "violations": violations_wire(doc.elements),
319	    }
320	    if link_source_revisions:
321	        # FED-01 polish: per-source current revisions so the UI can render
322	        # drift badges on pinned links without an extra round-trip.
323	        out["linkSourceRevisions"] = link_source_revisions
324	    return out
325	
326	
327	async def _resolve_link_source_revisions(
328	    session: AsyncSession, host_doc: Document
329	) -> dict[str, int]:
330	    """Look up the current revision of every distinct source UUID referenced
331	    by a ``link_model`` element in ``host_doc``. Missing sources are omitted
332	    from the result. Used by the FED-01 drift-badge UI."""
333	
334	    out: dict[str, int] = {}
335	    for elem in host_doc.elements.values():
336	        if not isinstance(elem, LinkModelElem):
337	            continue
338	        src_uuid_str = elem.source_model_id
339	        if src_uuid_str in out:
340	            continue
341	        try:
342	            src_uuid = UUID(src_uuid_str)
343	        except ValueError:
344	            continue
345	        src_row = await load_model_row(session, src_uuid)
346	        if src_row is None:
347	            continue
348	        try:
349	            src_doc = Document.model_validate(src_row.document)
350	        except Exception:
351	            continue
352	        out[src_uuid_str] = int(src_doc.revision)
353	    return out
354	
355	
356	async def _expand_host_links(
357	    session: AsyncSession,
358	    host_doc: Document,
359	    host_elements_wire: dict[str, Any],
360	) -> dict[str, Any]:
361	    """FED-01: resolve every ``link_model`` row's source document from DB and
362	    pass it through ``expand_links`` to inline transformed source elements.
363	
364	    Sources are loaded at their pinned revision when set (replayed via the
365	    undo stack), or at their current revision otherwise. Missing sources are
366	    skipped silently — the host is still authoritative.
367	    """
368	
369	    cache: dict[tuple[str, int | None], Document | None] = {}
370	
371	    async def _load_source(source_uuid_str: str, source_rev: int | None) -> Document | None:
372	        cache_key = (source_uuid_str, source_rev)
373	        if cache_key in cache:
374	            return cache[cache_key]
375	        try:
376	            source_uuid = UUID(source_uuid_str)
377	        except ValueError:
378	            cache[cache_key] = None
379	            return None
380	        src_row = await load_model_row(session, source_uuid)
381	        if src_row is None:
382	            cache[cache_key] = None
383	            return None
384	        current = Document.model_validate(src_row.document)
385	        if source_rev is None or source_rev == current.revision:
386	            cache[cache_key] = current
387	            return current
388	        # Replay backwards through the undo stack to land at the requested
389	        # revision (mirrors the diff endpoint's logic).
390	        try:
391	            doc_at = await _document_at_revision(session, source_uuid, current, source_rev)
392	        except HTTPException:
393	            cache[cache_key] = None
394	            return None
395	        cache[cache_key] = doc_at
396	        return doc_at
397	
398	    # Pre-load every link's source synchronously (the providers callable in
399	    # ``expand_links`` is sync; we resolve up-front).
400	    for elem in host_doc.elements.values():
401	        if isinstance(elem, LinkModelElem):
402	            await _load_source(elem.source_model_id, elem.source_model_revision)
403	
404	    def _provider(source_uuid_str: str, source_rev: int | None) -> Document | None:
405	        return cache.get((source_uuid_str, source_rev))
406	
407	    return expand_links(host_doc, host_elements_wire, _provider)
408	
409	
410	async def _document_at_revision(
411	    session: AsyncSession, model_id: UUID, current: Document, target_rev: int
412	) -> Document:
413	    """Reconstruct ``current`` rolled back to ``target_rev`` by replaying
414	    undo bundles in reverse-revision order. Returns a fresh ``Document``;
415	    the ``revision`` attribute is informational and may not equal
416	    ``target_rev`` after the engine bumps the counter — element state is
417	    what matters for diff.
418	    """
419	    if target_rev == current.revision:
420	        return clone_document(current)
421	    res = await session.execute(
422	        select(UndoStackRecord)
423	        .where(
424	            UndoStackRecord.model_id == model_id,
425	            UndoStackRecord.revision_after > target_rev,
426	            UndoStackRecord.revision_after <= current.revision,
427	        )
428	        .order_by(desc(UndoStackRecord.revision_after), desc(UndoStackRecord.id))
429	    )
430	    rolling = clone_document(current)
431	    for entry in res.scalars().all():
432	        ok, new_doc, _cmds, _viols, _code = try_commit_bundle(rolling, list(entry.undo_commands))
433	        if ok and new_doc is not None:
434	            rolling = new_doc
435	        else:
436	            raise HTTPException(
437	                status_code=409,
438	                detail=(
439	                    "Cannot reconstruct historical revision — undo replay failed at "
440	                    f"revision_after={entry.revision_after}"
441	                ),
442	            )
443	    return rolling
444

446	@api_router.get("/models/{model_id}/diff")
447	async def model_diff(
448	    model_id: UUID,
449	    fromRev: Annotated[int | None, Query(ge=1)] = None,  # noqa: N803 — wire-format alias
450	    toRev: Annotated[int | None, Query(ge=1)] = None,  # noqa: N803
451	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
452	) -> dict[str, Any]:
453	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
454	    if row is None:
455	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
456	    current = Document.model_validate(row.document)
457	
458	    to_rev = toRev if toRev is not None else current.revision
459	    from_rev = fromRev if fromRev is not None else max(1, to_rev - 1)
460	
461	    if from_rev > current.revision or to_rev > current.revision:
462	        raise HTTPException(
463	            status_code=400,
464	            detail=(
465	                f"Revision out of range: model is at revision {current.revision} "
466	                f"(fromRev={from_rev}, toRev={to_rev})."
467	            ),
468	        )
469	
470	    doc_to = await _document_at_revision(session, model_id, current, to_rev)
471	    doc_from = await _document_at_revision(session, model_id, current, from_rev)
472	
473	    elements_from = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc_from.elements.items()}
474	    elements_to = {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in doc_to.elements.items()}
475	
476	    diff = compute_element_diff(elements_from, elements_to)
477	    return {
478	        "modelId": str(model_id),
479	        "fromRevision": from_rev,
480	        "toRevision": to_rev,
481	        **diff,
482	    }

837	@api_router.get(
838	    "/models/{model_id}/schedules/{schedule_id}/table",
839	    response_model=None,
840	)
841	async def schedule_derived_table(
842	    model_id: UUID,
843	    schedule_id: str,
844	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
845	    fmt: Annotated[str, Query(alias="format")] = "json",
846	    columns: Annotated[str | None, Query(alias="columns")] = None,
847	    include_schedule_totals_csv: Annotated[bool, Query(alias="includeScheduleTotalsCsv")] = False,
848	) -> dict[str, Any] | PlainTextResponse:
849	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_id)
850	    if row is None:
851	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
852	    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
853	    try:
854	        payload = derive_schedule_table(doc, schedule_id)
855	    except ValueError as exc:
856	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(exc)) from exc
857	    if fmt.strip().lower() == "csv":
858	        export_payload = payload
859	        if columns and columns.strip():
860	            wanted = [c.strip() for c in columns.split(",") if c.strip()]
861	            if wanted:
862	                export_payload = schedule_payload_with_column_subset(payload, wanted)
863	        csv_body = schedule_payload_to_csv(
864	            export_payload,
865	            include_totals_csv=include_schedule_totals_csv,
866	        )
867	        safe = "".join(ch for ch in schedule_id if ch.isalnum() or ch in ("-", "_")) or "schedule"
868	        return PlainTextResponse(
869	            csv_body,
870	            media_type="text/csv; charset=utf-8",
871	            headers={"Content-Disposition": f'attachment; filename="{safe}.csv"'},
872	        )
873	    out = payload
874	    if columns and columns.strip():
875	        wanted = [c.strip() for c in columns.split(",") if c.strip()]
876	        if wanted:
877	            out = schedule_payload_with_column_subset(payload, wanted)
878	    return out
879

902	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
903	# FED-04 — IFC → shadow-model link import
904	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
905	
906	
907	class ImportIfcBody(BaseModel):
908	    """FED-04: payload for ``POST /api/models/{host_id}/import-ifc``.
909	
910	    Either ``file_text`` (inline IFC STEP) or ``file_path`` (server-side path
911	    readable by the FastAPI process) must be supplied. ``slug`` names the new
912	    shadow-model row; ``link_name`` is the host-side display name for the
913	    auto-created ``link_model`` element. Both have sensible defaults so a
914	    minimal request just sends the IFC bytes.
915	    """
916	
917	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
918	    file_text: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="fileText")
919	    file_path: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="filePath")
920	    slug: str = Field(default="ifc-import", min_length=1, max_length=128)
921	    link_name: str = Field(default="Linked IFC", alias="linkName")
922	
923	
924	@api_router.post("/models/{host_id}/import-ifc")
925	async def import_ifc_to_shadow_link(
926	    host_id: UUID,
927	    body: ImportIfcBody,
928	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
929	    hub: Hub = Depends(get_hub),
930	) -> dict[str, Any]:
931	    """FED-04: import an IFC file as a brand-new shadow bim-ai model + auto-
932	    create a ``link_model`` row in the host pointing at it.
933	
934	    Round-trip: parse IFC → ``authoritativeReplay_v0`` command bundle →
935	    apply to a fresh ``ModelRecord`` in the same project → run
936	    ``createLinkModel`` against the host. The shadow model is independent
937	    from then on (host edits never reach back into it; the host treats its
938	    elements as read-only renderable context per FED-01).
939	    """
940	
941	    from bim_ai.export_ifc import build_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_sketch_v0
942	    from bim_ai.engine import (
943	        try_apply_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_v0,
944	        try_commit,
945	    )
946	
947	    # Resolve host first so we can mirror its project_id onto the shadow.
948	    host_row = await load_model_row(session, host_id)
949	    if host_row is None:
950	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Host model not found")
951	
952	    # Read the IFC text. The endpoint accepts either inline text or a path.
953	    if body.file_text is not None:
954	        step_text = body.file_text
955	    elif body.file_path is not None:
956	        try:
957	            with open(body.file_path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
958	                step_text = fh.read()
959	        except OSError as exc:
960	            raise HTTPException(
961	                status_code=400, detail=f"Cannot read IFC file: {exc}"
962	            ) from exc
963	    else:
964	        raise HTTPException(
965	            status_code=400,
966	            detail="import-ifc requires either fileText or filePath in the request body",
967	        )
968	
969	    sketch = build_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_sketch_v0(step_text)
970	    if sketch.get("available") is not True:
971	        raise HTTPException(
972	            status_code=400,
973	            detail={
974	                "reason": "ifc_replay_unavailable",
975	                "ifcReason": sketch.get("reason"),
976	            },
977	        )
978	
979	    # 1. Create the shadow model row in the host's project.
980	    shadow_id = uuid4()
981	    shadow_doc: Document = Document(revision=1, elements={})  # type: ignore[arg-type]
982	    ensure_internal_origin(shadow_doc)
983	    ensure_sun_settings(shadow_doc)
984	
985	    # 2. Apply the replay bundle in-memory.
986	    ok, replayed_doc, applied_cmds, _viols, code = try_apply_kernel_ifc_authoritative_replay_v0(
987	        shadow_doc, sketch
988	    )
989	    if not ok or replayed_doc is None:
990	        raise HTTPException(
991	            status_code=400,
992	            detail={"reason": "ifc_replay_failed", "code": code},
993	        )
994	
995	    # 3. Persist the shadow model.
996	    shadow_row = ModelRecord(
997	        id=shadow_id,
998	        project_id=host_row.project_id,
999	        slug=body.slug,
1000	        revision=replayed_doc.revision,
1001	        document=document_to_wire(replayed_doc),
1002	    )
1003	    session.add(shadow_row)
1004	    try:
1005	        await session.flush()
1006	    except IntegrityError:
1007	        await session.rollback()
1008	        raise HTTPException(
1009	            status_code=409,
1010	            detail="Shadow model slug already exists for this project — pass a unique 'slug'",
1011	        ) from None
1012	
1013	    # 4. Build a createLinkModel command and apply it to the host.
1014	    suggested_position = {"xMm": 0.0, "yMm": 0.0, "zMm": 0.0}
1015	    host_doc = Document.model_validate(host_row.document)
1016	    create_link = {
1017	        "type": "createLinkModel",
1018	        "name": body.link_name,
1019	        "sourceModelId": str(shadow_id),
1020	        "positionMm": suggested_position,
1021	        "rotationDeg": 0.0,
1022	        "originAlignmentMode": "origin_to_origin",
1023	    }
1024	    try:
1025	        host_ok, new_host_doc, _cmd, host_viols, host_code = try_commit(host_doc, create_link)
1026	    except Exception as exc:
1027	        await session.rollback()
1028	        raise HTTPException(
1029	            status_code=400, detail=f"createLinkModel failed: {exc}"
1030	        ) from exc
1031	    if not host_ok or new_host_doc is None:
1032	        await session.rollback()
1033	        raise HTTPException(
1034	            status_code=409,
1035	            detail={
1036	                "reason": host_code,
1037	                "violations": [v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for v in host_viols],
1038	            },
1039	        )
1040	
1041	    # The new link_model element id is the only one missing from doc_before.
1042	    new_link_ids = set(new_host_doc.elements.keys()) - set(host_doc.elements.keys())
1043	    if len(new_link_ids) != 1:
1044	        await session.rollback()
1045	        raise HTTPException(
1046	            status_code=500,
1047	            detail="Internal: createLinkModel did not produce exactly one new element",
1048	        )
1049	    link_element_id = next(iter(new_link_ids))
1050	
1051	    # Persist the host. Keep the undo-stack record so the import is undoable.
1052	    host_row.document = document_to_wire(new_host_doc)  # type: ignore[assignment]
1053	    host_row.revision = new_host_doc.revision
1054	    await session.commit()
1055	
1056	    # Broadcast the host's delta so connected clients pick up the link.
1057	    try:
1058	        await hub.publish(
1059	            host_id,
1060	            {
1061	                "type": "delta",
1062	                "modelId": str(host_id),
1063	                "revision": new_host_doc.revision,
1064	            },
1065	        )
1066	    except Exception:
1067	        # Hub failures must not roll back the import.
1068	        pass
1069	
1070	    return {
1071	        "linkedModelId": str(shadow_id),
1072	        "linkElementId": link_element_id,
1073	        "suggestedLinkPosition": suggested_position,
1074	        "appliedReplayCommandCount": len(applied_cmds),
1075	        "shadowModelSlug": body.slug,
1076	    }
1077	
1078
